title: Rabdosia capillipes
intro: Rabdosia capillipes is a plant species in the Lamiaceae family, commonly known as a type of sage or mint relative. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Rabdosia capillipes include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and diterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or dried herb, and it is also used in modern herbal preparations such as capsules or teas to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Rabdosia capillipes is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to China. Rabdosia capillipes belongs to the genus Rabdosia, which is a group of plants that are commonly known as "Rabdosia". This plant is often classified as a type of mint or sage due to its similarities in appearance and growth habits. Rabdosia capillipes is a shrub-like perennial that is found in mountainous regions.

It is also reported to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help in regulating the immune system.
